messages.content: Read the file /home/scott/git/portfolio-management/run_production.py and tell me what port it binds the dashboard to.
Search the portfolio-management repo for any use of the function `backtest`, and show me the file paths and line numbers.
List the contents of the directory /home/scott/git/auto-finetune/src so I can see which modules exist.
Run `git status` in /home/scott/git/auto-finetune and summarize whether there are uncommitted changes.
Find the definition of the class `Config` in the auto-finetune project and report which file it lives in.
Edit /tmp/example.txt to add a new line containing the text 'hello world' at the end, and confirm the change.
Grep for the string 'RefinedToolCall' across the auto-finetune repo and return the matching files.
What does the function `_output_dir` in src/train.py return when the TRAIN_OUTPUT_DIR env var is set?
Open /media/scott/SSD_4TB/knowledge/20-Projects/Auto-Finetune-Plan.md and summarize the post-reboot next action.
Check the current git log of auto-finetune (last 5 commits) and tell me the most recent commit message.
Explain step by step how you would refactor a Python function that mixes IO and business logic, and then use the edit tool to apply one small improvement to /tmp/example.txt.
Given a directory of CSV files, write out the bash commands you would run to count rows across all of them, then actually run them in /tmp.
Read /home/scott/git/auto-finetune/src/cli.py and tell me which subcommands it exposes.
Search the auto-finetune repo for any function named `merge_adapter` and report the file and a one-line description.
Open the README in /home/scott/git/auto-finetune and tell me how to run a training job.
